results.report: A tough way to end what has been a brilliant first season. The Stags played some decent stuff on the ball but found themselves 2-0 down at the break, with the referee making his presence felt throughout. Stan O'Toole was then harshly sent off for a soft foul on the keeper — and that sparked the free fall. The goals kept coming, and Logan McDonald followed him down the tunnel later on, along with one of Dar United's men, as the game completely fell apart. The scoreline is hard to take but it doesn't define what's been a fantastic debut campaign in the Middlesex County Sunday League. Heads high. 🦌

Another tough battle against a good side. The Stags started brilliantly — Garnham curled in a stunning freekick to open the scoring before adding a second to make it 2-0. Garnham thought he had his hat-trick soon after but the effort was ruled offside. Ryefield clawed one back with a well-taken header from a set-piece. A nasty challenge on Carmody forced him off, with Harper stepping up between the sticks to help the Stags see out the half. The Stags pushed hard in the second half but a cruel bobble just as Harper was about to clear saw it bounce over his foot and into the net to level things up. Few chances either side after that, and the game ended with Ryefield's keeper seeing red. The Stags can be proud of how hard they battled for the full ninety minutes. 🦌

Was always gonna be a tough game against a good side fighting for the league title. Playing on Astro for the first time too. The Stags started well with a few chances, even a goal disallowed for offside. But as the game went on the gaps started to show and the hosts took a quick 3-0 lead. A reshuffle at half time did calm the Stags down and allowed us to get on the ball and create chances which we did. However as game went on the chances went a begging, even a penalty which was missed again by Reggie. Time was running and the hosts got another one to seal the deal. Not our finest in the shining sun.
results.reportTitle: Stags come up short against title challengers Kensington

results.report: Jubilation, Justification and Justice, a frantic morning today at Coney Green. Stags started strong with some nice neat passing, Reggie placed a long free kick on the head of Zak, who headed it back across to Lewis to punish the sloppy defence. 5 minutes later KP flip flops through the midfield before laying off Mason who rifled home from range. 2nd half Stags started flat after some substitutions, and we were punished, 3 goals in quick succession and that was all she wrote. 3-2 Samurai. Steve makes more changes and brings in Zak who changes the game. Corner comes in and finds Tyler's head, with a very fortunate deflection that found the net. 5 minutes later another long free kick from Reggie found Zak at the back post, keeper had no interest in attempting a save so the ball found the net. 10 minutes of solid defending saw the win out, 4-3, job done!
results.reportTitle: Jubilation! Stags win incredible 4-3 thriller against Samurai

results.report: A must win for the Stags today. Game started fast with Stan O hitting a nice curled shot into the far left corner. However the home side came straight back winning a penalty after Jack H left a leg to the striker to fall over but Jack C stepped up to save it again. The pressure did continue though and the home side found their goal after some good play down the left side. The rest of the game was very cagey and was only won when Tyler lobbed the keeper from 40 yards out. Not the Stags best performance but 3 points is 3 points. 🦌
results.reportTitle: Tyler's 40-yard lob seals must-win away day

results.report: The Stags started the game on the back foot due to storm Dave having a field day. We did eventually work out how to fight the wind but by that point it was halftime. Stags came out with the wind with them but we didn't capitalise and 20 minutes into the half, the host went 1-0 up due to a free header from a corner. Stags could not get anywhere near the opposition box and only had a goal mouth scramble at the end before the ref blew up. Bad day at the office.
results.reportTitle: Storm Dave blows Stags off course at Old Southall

results.report: Another big win was needed if we had any hope of the title. Stags started really well, popping the ball about and creating a few half chances but nothing to trouble the keeper. The half got more scrappy as it went on but neither side had any real efforts on goal. The second half started bright and not long into it a rattling volley from Tyler smacked off the crossbar and was finished up by Mason on the rebound. A deserved goal for a big shift by the Stags. However we couldn't continue that pressure and towards the back end of the game, the visitors got a goal back. Last 5 minutes was looking rough for the Stags but some great saves from Jack C kept us in it. The 3rd 1-1 draw in 4 games.
results.reportTitle: Stags frustrated at home as Maida Villa take a point

results.report: A test of what we wanted this season to be today. A strong performance and win keeps us in the race for promotion but lose and it might be season done. The Stags started really well, playing some good football and were rewarded with a goal thanks to a smart finish from Billy. The second half also started well with Mason cutting inside to win a penalty, however Reggie couldn't convert so it stayed 1-0. A very controlling second half saw the second goal come when some great pressing work from Lewis knocked the ball across the goal for Gam to end his goal drought. A nice ball through the middle and a smart finish gave the home side something to cheer about but the game ended 2-1 to the Stags. A very similar game to the reverse at home.
results.reportTitle: Back to winning ways — Billy and Gam sink Jugg Boyz

results.report: Probably the most boring game of football ever. Stags looked to bounce back from their cup defeat to Ryefield with a game against Kodak who we drew 3-3 in the 90th minute last time out. Stags started really well with some great chances and it didn't take long for Stan O to find the net to make it 1-0. Then that was about it for the next 50 minutes of play. Nothing really happened until a ball into the box caught the Stags out and Kodak made it 1-1. Then like before nothing happened and the game ended like it had never really happened. The most exciting bit was when Jack H nearly did a repeat of the goal we conceded last week as he knocked the ball past Jack C who was coming to claim it but luckily Jack showed good speed to get back and clear it off the line. Flat today from the Stags but onto the next. 🦌
results.reportTitle: O'Toole earns a point in a tight home draw with Kodak

results.competition: Patrons Division 4 Challenge Cup Semi Final
results.report: Semi Final with a team that have beaten us before, was always gonna be tough. Stags went a goal down early but found their feet through till half time. Started the second half strong and when we were awarded a penalty for a handball in the box, we knew it was an opportunity we had to take. Reggie stepped up with a brilliant pen to get us back in it. The game got scrappy and looked to go to penalties but a lapse of concentration from the Stags gave the hosts the lead. Few efforts late on to equalise but nothing came of it. The game then got away from the Stags after 2 more goals came from the home team. A really great effort from everybody, a semi final in our first season is something to be proud of. A good game of football and we wish Ryefield all the best in the final. Few handbags at the end with one red card shown to one of them.
results.reportTitle: Cup run ends at Ryefield in the semi-final

results.report: Needed a win today to stay in the title race. First half the Stags were brilliant. Won every first and second ball, battled really well and our efforts were rewarded as KP snuck home a through ball. Saw the first half out with a good performance ready for the second half. Again started the half well, looking for the second to try kill the game off. Lots of chances from set pieces but nothing to show for it and as the game went on it was looking more likely the home side would get one back. And they did. A depleted Stags team then looked for a winner but left it too late. A few glorious chances came however but no one could find the net. Made a tough game out of it when it didn't need to be and it's 2 points dropped.
results.reportTitle: Two points dropped as Stags draw at Chiswick

results.report: As the rain was pouring down, there was doubt that this fixture would go ahead but despite the bog we played on. Game started fast with a few chances going either way before a great cross found an onside Gam who first time poked it past the keeper to give us the lead. Despite the score line we were not playing our best but another long ball from a goal kick put Stan O through to slot home for the second. The second half went quick with neither side really looking strong but then a late penalty courtesy of Cody's back tackle, got the away side back in the game. However the Stags held out for the big 3 points on a very wet and boggy day.
results.reportTitle: Stags hold firm in the rain to seal three points against Jugg Boyz

results.report: A boggy affair this one. We started the game strong, going 1 up through some great team pressing and a smart Stan O finish. The home side picked up and found the equaliser shortly and as the second half started, they made it 2-1. Stags heads didn't drop as Zak got on the end of a lovely Gam corner to level us up. The game ticked on and looked like the points would be shared but an absolute thunderbolt into the top left from Mason stole the game. Then Billy found himself through on goal and calmly slotted home to secure the points. A great away win and the first in our new kit. 🦌
results.reportTitle: First win in the new kit — Stags put four past Samurai away

results.competition: Patrons Division 4 Challenge Cup Quarter Final
results.report: Cup quarter-final time. The biggest game of our history. Game started tense, both teams having half chances but nothing to separate them going into half time. As the game ticked on, it looked inevitable that one goal would win this game and unfortunately the away side found it through a controversial offside second ball after a save from Jack C. Stags heads didn't drop though. After a flurry of balls into the box, a defensive header found its way to Max about 40 yards out. BOOM! Max rifled one into the top left giving us hope and maybe a shot at glory. However the game finished 1-1 so it was down to penalties. Reggie stepped up first. Snuck it under the keeper. Jack C then makes a great save to stop their first taker. Tyler, Gam and Stan T then all slotted home to give us a winning shot. Logan slams it home to give us the win. LIMBS. What a way to go through to a semi-final.
results.reportTitle: Stags through to the semis on penalties in cup thriller against Moor Mead

results.report: First game of 2026 with a 7 week break between our last 2 games, wasn't gonna be easy. A really poor start from the Stags meant we found ourselves 3-0 down inside the opening 20 minutes. Steve gave a passionate halftime team talk which seemed to put some spirit back in the Stags hearts as we started the second half better. Took us a while to find our attacking groove but then after some tidy dribbling and a neat pass across goal by Tyler, Jack N struck the ball home to give us a lifeline. Stags continued to battle well however couldn't follow up with anything and the clock ticked into the 90th minute where Ryefield found their sucker punch that ended all hope of a comeback. Need to start games better. 😡
results.reportTitle: Ryefield end Stags' winter break with a reality check

results.report: Wow wow wow, what a game of football. The Stags last game of 2025 and it didn't disappoint. Started the game off really strong, Logan firing a long range missile into the top right to give us the lead. Then things quickly went wrong. Cody was shown a yellow card which meant he was in the sin bin for 10 mins. Following that KP put in a vicious flying elbow and booted the guys head in (I'm joking, he accidentally tripped him up on a counter) however the ref must have seen it like that as he issued a straight red card. Down to 9. The second half was gonna be tough for the Stags but we fought well. However the man advantage got the better of us as the away side netted 2 quick goals to put them in front. However we don't go down that easy as from a rare corner the ball fell to Lewis who tucked it home to equalise. Then after some brilliant running from Tyler, the ball found its way to Jack Garnham about 10 yards out. Only one outcome. Jack slotted it home. LIMBS. From 2-1 down to 3-2 and it got better. Some tidy play from Stanley and Mason opened up Lewis to bag his second of the game. After the world (and the ref) threw everything against us, the Stags come out on top. That's just what we do. UTFS
results.reportTitle: Nine men, four goals — Stags end 2025 in style

results.report: Playing the exact same team again, we knew what they were about. Game started fast with the Stags having a few chances before Tyler slapped a rocket into the top left after some good pressing. The Stags were in control but unfortunately couldn't see the game out to half time as the away side got back into it after some poor defending from the Stags. However that only fuelled the team as the floodgates opened in the second half with Jack Garnham scoring 4 goals to give us the comfortable win. Special mention to the last goal with an excellent passing play down the right hand side to open Jack Norman for a perfect cross with Jack Garnham getting on the end of it to grab his fourth. A deserved 3 points as we push for top spot in the league.
results.reportTitle: Garnham bags four as Stags run riot against Hounslow

results.report: The game started slowly, both teams just working each other out. It was a new system for the Stags, a 3-5-2, so adapting quickly was key. After a flurry of set pieces, we finally took the lead from a second ball after a corner that was chipped into the back post by Zak and headed in by Logan. The Stags were on top but a harsh penalty decision gave the visitors a way back into the game, but luckily Jack was in fine form and made an excellent save to give the Stags the lead heading into the break. We started the second half strong, finding another goal through Tyler after some scrappy defending. Then came another terrible penalty decision after a fantastic challenge by Cody which the away side managed to finish this time. Stags were defending brilliantly and switched back to the fabled 4 back to see us out. Then the 3rd penalty of the game came when Joe was shoved down inside the box. Reggie stepped up and buried it, giving us a bit of calm and ultimately seeing the game out for a great win in the new kit. Hopefully this can be a nice start to a good cup run. UTFS 🦌
results.reportTitle: Stags through to the cup quarters with a convincing win

results.report: A close one that went the wrong way. Garnham and Murphy both scored but Dar United nicked it with a third late on. Murphy was excellent throughout and rightly took the MOTM.
results.reportTitle: Stags fall narrowly at Dar United in a tight contest

results.report: Two goals to the good and the Stags couldn't hold on. Garnham and Pusey both scored but Chiswick worked hard to level it up. Pusey took home MOTM despite the result.
results.reportTitle: Stags pegged back twice in frustrating home draw

results.report: The Stags progress to the Patrons Division 4 Challenge Cup quarter-final without kicking a ball. Maida Villa were unable to fulfil the fixture and the tie was awarded to Hillingdon. On to the quarters.
results.reportTitle: Stags progress in the cup as Maida Villa default

results.report: Back to winning ways and then some. Garnham bagged a brace with Pusey and Richards also on the scoresheet in a commanding away performance. Rogers put in a solid shift at the back to earn MOTM.
results.reportTitle: Stags bounce back with four away from home

results.competition: Roger Perry Junior Open Cup Round 2
results.report: The Stags bowed out of the Roger Perry Junior Open Cup in round two. Garnham pulled one back but West Drayton's quality showed throughout. Burlikowski earned MOTM for his efforts between the sticks.
results.reportTitle: Cup exit as West Drayton prove too strong

results.report: A rough one. Maida Villa were clinical on the day and the Stags couldn't find a way back into it after a difficult first half. Bourke grabbed the consolation. Heads up and move on.
results.reportTitle: Tough afternoon at Maida Villa

results.report: A clinical Sunday morning from the Stags. Bourke, Carmody and O'Toole (with a brace) did the damage as Hillingdon kept it tight at the back. A proper team performance all round.
results.reportTitle: Four goals and a clean sheet as Stags dismantle Old Southall

results.report: The Stags went toe-to-toe with Kodak in a feisty away draw. Garnham, Norman and Thompson all found the net but the hosts kept pegging them back. A point away from home, though the lads will know they had more in the tank.
results.reportTitle: Battling point at Kodak after end-to-end draw

results.report: The perfect start to the season. Five different scorers — Bourke, Garnham, Henry, Richards and Thompson — as the Stags ran out comfortable winners at home. A real statement of intent from the lads.
results.reportTitle: Stags open with a statement — five goals in a dominant home display
